title: rosa elliptica subvar. gypsophila
intro: Rosa elliptica subvar. gypsophila, also known as the sweetbriar rose, is a fragrant flowering shrub native to Europe and Western Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Rosa elliptica subvar. gypsophila include its ability to aid in digestion and relieve menstrual cramps, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Rosa elliptica subvar. gypsophila can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues.
